title: Fix example; the --name option does not accept a parameter

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jberkus* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **mheon** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @mheon` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This doesn't seem right - `podman create --name` definitely takes an argument?

@mheon `podman-generate-systemd --name` does not. You can read the rest of the docs, or you can test it yourself.

Personally, I would like it to take a parameter, but it currently doesn't.
@vrothberg PTAL, it would be nice if --name and --name=value worked, but this does not work in cobra.

> @vrothberg PTAL, it would be nice if --name and --name=value worked, but this does not work in cobra.

You can do this with `flags.Lookup("name").NoOptDefVal = "SOMEVALUE"`. If you set this you can do both `--name` and `--name=value`. The only problem is that you cannot set `NoOptDefVal` to an empty string.
https://play.golang.org/p/aXEkhTA_6c1

Let's first agree whether there's a problem and which it is.

By default, `podman generate systemd` will generate units (and file names) based on the IDs of containers and pods. Using `--name` uses the names instead of the IDs.  Note that this is purely a boolean switch.

The change here is wrong as `podman create --name=xxx` works and the parameter is mandatory.

@jberkus, I hope this explanation makes sense.

> @vrothberg PTAL, it would be nice if --name and --name=value worked, but this does not work in cobra.

I _really_ do not like it due to the ambiguity of  `--name=foobar` on a POD; we cannot control the names of the containers.  At the moment, it only changes the mode between using either IDs or names and I think we should keep it that way.
I guess the question is what is @jberkus goal?
Oh!  I misread the example as being podman generate systemd.  I get it now, closing.
